title: Envelope
description: Top-level WebSocket frame definitions. All frames are JSON objects. Client-to-server frames are *action envelopes*; server-to-client frames are *event envelopes*. Each is discriminated by `action` (client) or `type` (server).

ErrorObject.title: ErrorObject
ErrorObject.description: A structured error descriptor. Used in error events and nested inside event data payloads.

ActionEnvelope.title: ActionEnvelope
ActionEnvelope.description: Base shape for every client-to-server WebSocket frame. The `action` field is a snake_case verb that selects the handler. `requestId` is chosen by the client and echoed back on all related server events so the client can correlate responses to pending requests.

requestId.description: Client-generated correlation ID. Will be echoed back on all related server events. Should be unique per in-flight request. If omitted the server may generate one, but correlating responses becomes the client's problem.

EventEnvelope.title: EventEnvelope
EventEnvelope.description: Base shape for every server-to-client WebSocket frame. `type` is a snake_case noun that identifies the event. `requestId` echoes the originating action's `requestId` where applicable (may be absent for unsolicited events such as `keepalive`). `status` uses HTTP-like codes: 202 = accepted/in-progress, 200 = final success, 4xx/5xx = error.

type.description: Identifies which event this frame carries.

requestId.description: Echoes the `requestId` from the originating action. Absent for unsolicited server-push events.

status.description: HTTP-like status code. 202 = acknowledged/in-progress; 200 = final success; 4xx/5xx = error conditions.

message.description: Optional human-readable status description (used in `immediate_response` acks).

data.description: Event-specific payload. Shape is defined by the per-event schema in `events/`.

node.description: Name of the workflow node that produced this event. Present on `stream_chunk` events.

token.description: A streamed LLM token. Present on `stream_token` events.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch milliseconds. Present on `pong` events and optionally on others.
